automated: verify_test.cjs test_generate_valid_script
assert hasMeta=true for valid script
assert new Function(script) succeeds
assert fs.existsSync(filePath) after write
automated: verify_test.cjs test_generate_no_meta
assert hasMeta=false for 'console.log(1)'
code_trace: index.ts meta check throws 'Script must contain a meta export'
automated: test with script='invalid {{{'
verify_test.cjs Round 1: 'invalid {{{' has no meta → rejected by meta check, not syntax check
original test case assumed syntax error would be caught, but meta check runs first
script needs 'const meta' to pass meta check and reach syntax validation
test_execution.evidence: verify_test.cjs Round 1: meta check blocks path to syntax check for scripts without meta

execute_steps: automated: fixed test script to include meta: 'const meta = { name: "bad", ... }; invalid {{{'
assert hasMeta=true → passes meta check
assert new Function(script) throws → syntax check catches error
code_trace: index.ts line ~667 catch → throw new Error('Syntax error in script: ...')
test_execution.evidence: verify_test.cjs Round 2 PASS — SYNTAX_ERR_SCRIPT has meta but invalid syntax

existing.find(wf => wf.name === 'batch-review') finds conflict
automated: verify_test.cjs test_generate_auto_mkdir
cleanup removes entire test dir, only SAVED_DIR created
mkdirSync(TMP_DIR, { recursive: true }) creates .tmp from scratch
assert fs.existsSync(TMP_DIR) and written file
automated: verify_test.cjs test_save_normal
create .tmp/review-src.js
renameSync to saved/review-src.js
assert dest exists, tmp gone
automated: verify_test.cjs test_save_as_rename
renameSync .tmp/review-src.js → saved/batch-review-v2.js
assert renamed file exists
automated: verify_test.cjs test_save_dest_exists
create both .tmp/demo.js and saved/demo.js
assert fs.existsSync(destPath) === true → would reject
code_trace: saveWorkflow uses renameSync (synchronous fs metadata change)
code_trace: Worker threads run in separate child_process — no shared file handles
renameSync only changes directory entry, does not affect open file descriptors on POSIX
test_execution.evidence: code_trace: commands.ts saveWorkflow + Worker in separate process

execute_steps: code_trace: commands.ts list case — loadWorkflows() returns items with .source field
code_trace: workflows.map(wf => `[${wf.source}] ${wf.name} — ...`)
ISSUE: discovered MF2 from code review — original dedup had `if (wf.available)` filter that silently dropped unavailable scripts
unavailable scripts (e.g. broken imports) would not appear in list despite being on disk
test_execution.evidence: code review MF2 identified: dedup filter dropped unavailable workflows

execute_steps: code_trace: config-loader.ts MF2 fix — removed `if (wf.available)` filter
all workflows now included in mergedMap regardless of available status
list handler shows [source] tag for all items including unavailable
test_execution.evidence: commit 1b13bb1 fixed MF2 — dedup no longer filters unavailable
